name: embedding_retrieval_domain
description: A compact DOMAIN corpus for the embedding_retrieval suite (SUITE-EMB): abstracted, PII-free knowledge about the fleet's own domain (self-hosted model serving, retrieval, and the code knowledge graph), the kind of content a domain corpus such as an Engram/KG-derived labeled set would hold. Measures a candidate embedding model's DOMAIN FIT; a large negative delta versus the public baseline is a domain-mismatch flag, not a bad model. All text is abstracted prose with no private data.

docs.text: A model-profiling suite measures a candidate along one capability axis and writes each derived metric as a dimension score row keyed by model and backend.
An embedding model maps text to a dense vector so that semantically similar passages land close together under cosine similarity.
Retrieval quality is scored with precision at k, recall at k, mean reciprocal rank, and rank-discounted gain normalized by the ideal ordering.
A code knowledge graph indexes symbols, files, and references across a repository so an agent can ground an answer in the actual source.
An inference proxy routes each request to a backend by model tag, lazily starting a serve on demand and stopping it when idle to free accelerator memory.
A profiling sweep loads one model at a time, runs its assigned suites, records the results, and evicts the model before loading the next candidate.
The catalog tracks coverage as a matrix of model by test type by task category, where an untested cell is a first-class not-run entry rather than a blank.
A corpus loader reads its query and document set from a configured directory and rejects any file whose text trips the private-data gate.

queries.text: How is retrieval quality measured for an embedding model?
What does the code knowledge graph index?
How does the proxy decide where to run a model and free memory?
How does a profiling sweep handle one model at a time?
How does the catalog represent an untested capability?
